learningLog: Tournament '12.2-ember-speed' ran 120 games across 3 matchup(s). Pairs: HellA=(deck:ember-aggro,bot:experimental/2026-04-19-hellfire), HellB=(deck:ember-aggro,bot:experimental/2026-04-19-hellfire)

Matchup HellB vs HellB: deck 'ember-aggro' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-hellfire' versus deck 'ember-aggro' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-hellfire'. HellB won 1/40 (BWins:0,Draws:39, 2%). Self-mirror — indicates raw seed-driven variance and whether the pairing is stable given the RNG. Average game length: 428 steps.

Across all matchups, 'HellB' (deck:ember-aggro,bot:experimental/2026-04-19-hellfire) outperformed 'HellA' (deck:ember-aggro,bot:experimental/2026-04-19-hellfire). Pattern: the top pair's bot profile is worth studying as a candidate to pair with similar decks; the weakest pair suggests either a weight-table mismatch or a deck that disagrees with the bot's scoring priorities.
Observation: Close race: 'HellB' edges out runner-up by 1 points — consider more games per matchup.

LEARNING HINT: When choosing a bot for a new deck, prefer profiles whose matched decks in this tournament share archetype tags (aggro/control/midrange) with the new deck. Avoid pairing a utility bot tuned for defence with an aggro deck — it will underweight tempo plays.
